q.en: What is Charly's real name, and what does she prefer to be called?
q.zh: Charly的真名是什么，她更喜欢被叫什么？

options.en: Her name is Charlotte according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 她叫Charlotte（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Her real name is Charlemagne, but she prefers her nickname Charly
options.zh: 真名是Charlemagne，但她更喜欢昵称Charly

explain.en: Charly says, 'I didn't pick it; my parents did... I did pick my nickname from it: Charly.' Her full name is Charlemagne.
explain.zh: Charly说：“这个名字不是我选的，是我父母起的……但我从里面选了自己的昵称：Charly。”她的全名是Charlemagne。

q.en: Why does Dad say Charly needs to 'turn over a new leaf'?
q.zh: 爸爸为什么说Charly需要“翻过新的一页”？

options.en: Because she failed fourth grade according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 因为她四年级没及格（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Because she is now ten (double digits) and needs to be more responsible
options.zh: 因为她现在十岁（两位数年龄），需要更负责任

explain.en: Dad says, 'It means you are getting to an age when you need to be more responsible for your actions. It's time you turned over a new leaf.' He means now that she is ten.
explain.zh: 爸爸说：“这意味着你到了一个需要为行为更负责任的年龄。是时候翻过新的一页了。”他指的是Charly如今十岁。

q.en: When Dad says he 'got up on the wrong side of the bed,' what does Charly first think?
q.zh: 当爸爸说“got up on the wrong side of the bed（起床踩错了边）”时，Charly起初怎么想？

options.en: She thinks he had a bad dream according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 她以为他做了噩梦（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: She thinks there is a real right and wrong side of the bed and tries to move it
options.zh: 她以为床真的有正确和错误的一边，于是试图移动床

explain.en: Charly doesn't understand the saying, thinking 'I never even knew there was a right side of a bed,' so she goes to investigate and tries to move the bed.
explain.zh: Charly听不懂这句俗语，心想“我从来不知道床还有正确的一边”，于是去查看并试图移动床。

q.en: What did Aunt Dee bring Charly from her travels?
q.zh: Aunt Dee从旅行中给Charly带了什么？

options.en: A bag of chips according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter before the main events begin
options.zh: 一袋薯片（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）（在主要事件发生之前）

options.en: A snow globe from Play World Amusement Park in Sandusky, Ohio, with a model of 'The Rattler' roller coaster
options.zh: 一个来自俄亥俄州Sandusky市Play World游乐园的雪景球，里面是“The Rattler”过山车的模型

explain.en: Aunt Dee 'handed me a snow globe from the Play World Amusement Park in Sandusky, Ohio. Inside was a model of "The Rattler," one of the oldest wooden roller coasters in the United States.'
explain.zh: Aunt Dee“递给我一个来自俄亥俄州Sandusky市Play World游乐园的雪景球。里面是‘The Rattler’的模型，它是美国最古老的木质过山车之一。”

q.en: According to Aunt Dee, what did Charly's mom Francesca do that showed she 'grabbed the bull by the horns'?
q.zh: 根据Aunt Dee的说法，Charly的妈妈Francesca做了什么，说明她“grabbed the bull by the horns（勇敢行动）”？

options.en: She was a teacher according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她是一名老师（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: She was a firefighter (brave and take-charge)
options.zh: 她是一名消防员（勇敢、敢作敢为）

explain.en: Aunt Dee says Charly's mom 'grabbed the bull by the horns!' and Charly replies, 'I knew Mom had been a firefighter'—showing her brave, take-charge spirit.
explain.zh: Aunt Dee说Charly的妈妈“抓住了牛角！”（比喻勇敢行动），而Charly回答“我知道妈妈曾是消防员”——展现了她勇敢、敢作敢为的精神。

options.en: Turns around in a circle according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 绕着圈转（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines resolutions as 'firm decisions to do something.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将resolutions定义为“下定决心去做某事”。

q.en: What does Charly decide she wants to create for the New Year?
q.zh: Charly决定要在新年创造出什么？

options.en: Something beautiful that puts a smile on everyone's face
options.zh: 美好的东西，让大家脸上都露出笑容

options.en: A snow globe collection according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 雪景球收藏（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Charly tells Aunt Dee, 'I would like to create something beautiful that puts a smile on everyone's face.'
explain.zh: Charly对Aunt Dee说：“我想创造出美好的东西，让大家脸上都露出笑容。”

q.en: What is the title of Part Two of the series?
q.zh: 这个系列的第二部标题是什么？

options.en: Raining Cats, Dogs, and Other Animals
options.zh: 《天上下猫下狗，还有其他动物》

explain.en: The book ends, 'Find out next time in "Charly's New Year's Revolution."'
explain.zh: 书结尾写道：“下次请在《Charly的新年变革》中寻找答案。”

q.en: How does Charly describe being mature, the way Gattie says it?
q.zh: Charly如何描述“成熟”——按Gattie的说法？

explain.en: Gattie says mature as 'ma-toor,' which rhymes with door; Charly notes it is not 'ma-chewer,' which rhymes with sewer.
explain.zh: Gattie把mature说成“ma-toor”，和door押韵；Charly指出它不是“ma-chewer”，和sewer押韵。

q.en: Why does the author tell this story in Charly's funny first-person voice?
q.zh: 作者为什么用Charly有趣的第一人称讲述这个故事？

options.en: To entertain and let readers see a child's playful, mixed-up view of grown-up sayings
options.zh: 为了娱乐读者，并让读者看到一个孩子对照字面理解大人俗语时那种俏皮又搞混的视角

options.en: To teach how to fly planes
To write a history of roller coasters according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了写一部过山车史（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The story is told in Charly's funny first-person voice, letting readers enjoy a child's playful, mixed-up understanding of grown-up sayings.
explain.zh: 故事用Charly有趣的第一人称讲述，让读者领略一个孩子对照字面理解大人俗语时那种俏皮又搞混的视角。

prompt.en: Imagine you are Charly writing your own New Year's resolution list. Write three resolutions and explain why each one matters to you.
prompt.zh: 想象你是Charly，写下你自己的新年决心清单。写出三条决心，并解释每一条对你为什么重要。

en: Include at least one resolution about being more responsible (like Charly's list).
Include one creative or kind goal, such as making someone smile.
zh: 至少包含一条关于更负责任的决心（像Charly的清单那样）。
包含一条有创意或善良的目标，比如让别人微笑。
